title: Configuration
description: Full HoundOptions, processor, and retry reference

blocks.text: Pass options to new HoundApp() (or Hound.create() if you only need the runtime) to configure the instance. HoundApp accepts the same HoundOptions and additionally exposes typed defineJob and management:

rows: Shared app context passed to all handlers
Bearer token required by the HTTP gateway
Directories to scan for *.job.ts files on start

blocks.text: The two generics on HoundApp<TApp, TJobMap> propagate type-safety end-to-end: hound.emit / emitAsync / emitBatch / emitAndWait / on are narrowed to HoundJobMap, defineJob handlers receive a typed ctx.emit family, and ctx is the shape of TApp. Generate HoundJobMap with deno task codegen — see Defining Jobs.
Fine-tune the processor with the processor sub-option:

rows: How often the processor polls for new jobs
How long before a stalled job is reclaimed by the Reaper
How long completed and failed job records are kept
Max log entries stored per job
Queue ordering — lower number = higher priority

blocks.text: Set default retry behaviour in processor.retry. These defaults can be overridden per handler or per emit:
Jobs that exhaust all retry attempts are written to a state key queues:{queue}:{jobId}:failed:{execId} and remain inspectable via the management API:

There is no separate dead-letter queue — failed jobs live in the regular state-key namespace and respect processor.jobStateTtlSeconds. A first-class DLQ with separate retention and bulk replay may be reintroduced as a feature; see ROADMAP.md.
